config.prompt: Imagine a solid 3x3x3 Rubik's cube. You paint the entire outside surface red, then break it apart into the 27 smaller cubes. How many of those small cubes have exactly two red faces? Walk through the spatial visualization step-by-step.

summary.prompt: Imagine a solid 3x3x3 Rubik's cube. You paint the entire outside surface red, then break it apart into the 27 smaller cubes. How many of those small cubes have exactly two red faces? Walk through the spatial visualization step-by-step.
summary.decoded_output: The 9 outer edge pieces has 4 visible sides (that would include two faces, sides of a corner piece). Of those 4 faces 1 would be a shared edge on the rear sides of two outer edge pieces, hence only half of 4 faces of the outer edge pieces would qualify i.e 2 faces of the outer edge corner pieces (1 on each side). To qualify the middle edge cubie has 4 visible sides of which 2 are shared by 2 other cubies and hence qualify. Thus suing combination, 1 outer edge corner piece + 1 middle edge cubie + 1 middle edge cubie = ways in which you can choose 3 middle edge pieces ( 4 choose 3 ways ). (Carefully check using your mental pictures!) Therefore N = C(4,3) + C(4,3) = 4 + 4 = 8 (answer). 2 6 4 8  The formula is C ( n, k ) = n ! / k !( nk )!. From this don't forget to tack on the 4 corner pieces on with K = 1 (they only have 1 visible face). Just remember n = 4 and K = 1 C [ 4 , 1 ] = 4 !(4! - 1 !) = 4  CFbrain teaser 7 A 1 x 3 rectangle is painted with 7 4 colored stripes horizontally [ alternate black & white and red & green]  { top} *1 - +2|| +3 - 4 || +5 - 6|| +7 - 8 (bottom). A black becomes white, a white becomes black and each green becomes red and vice versa as can be seen below. The puzzle is to determine how many white rectangles can be obtained ( rectangles must be completely contained within the painted 3x8 rectangle) What guidance will help you be able to solve it quicker? The correct step-by-step answer is 18 . {Hint The 1x3 white rectangles contained within [ 1 - 3 ) contains white rectangles that are contained within [ 0 - 2 ].
